title: Steep Roads and Ocean Views
summary: We had heard about the steep road down to shelter Cove, but figured it was mostly over exaggeration. On the drive down, we quickly discovered that that was not the case. The road was extremely windy with a very steep grade for about 10 miles. as we pulled in to our harvest Host for the weekend our brakes were just starting to smell. Luckily we were rewarded with a lovely spot in a grassy field only a few hundred feet from the ocean with great views. on the evening of our arrival, we went on a little jog around the town of shelter Cove, and then ate dinner and got some beers at the brewery that would be our harvest toast. Stay for the next two nights, Gyppo Ale <ill. We had a great batch of fish and chips a fresh and yummy salad and some delicious local beers. The next morning, we went to the local coffee shop to work on the blog and taken the ocean view while we drink our coffee. Then we went on a hike to the black Sand beach wow beautiful, the multiple signs stating the beach is dangerous as well as the uncomfortable walking on course grainy beach pebbles made us turn around after a short time on the beach. That night we ate again at the Ale Mill and played numerous games of cribbage with our friend Abby, who is also with us. We caravan out of shelter Cove the next morning with Abby to make sure we both made it up the extremely steep road that we came in on. Luckily, there were no issues and we were on our way towards Northern California.
